title: Error::DataLost carries the NVM ID — defensible against collapsing to a generic error
description: nvm_overwrite deletes the old object then adds the new one. If the add fails after a successful delete the data is permanently gone. The dedicated Error::DataLost { id } variant surfaces the ID of the lost object, which is the piece of information callers need to decide how to recover (e.g. re-create from backup, alert an operator). A contributor might suggest collapsing this into the existing Error::Ffi variant to reduce enum size. That would lose the ID and the semantic distinction between 'transport error' and 'NVM object is permanently gone.' The dedicated variant is correct — callers matching on Error::Ffi cannot determine whether data was lost.
